questions.q: In Hindu tradition, what kind of being is King Bali, the generous ruler famed for his sacrifice?
questions.explanation: Bali is celebrated as a righteous and generous Asura (Daitya) king.

questions.q: Which avatar of Vishnu approached King Bali to ask for three paces of land?
questions.explanation: Vishnu came to Bali as Vamana, the dwarf Brahmin, in his fifth avatar.

questions.q: How many paces of land did the dwarf Vamana request from King Bali?
questions.explanation: Vamana humbly asked Bali for only three paces of land.

questions.q: King Bali was the grandson of which famous devotee of Vishnu?
questions.explanation: Bali was the grandson of Prahlada, the great devotee saved by Narasimha.

questions.q: To which realm was King Bali finally granted lordship after surrendering everything?
questions.explanation: Bali was given lordship over Patala, the netherworld, for his honesty.

questions.q: For which virtue is King Bali most celebrated in the Vamana episode?
options: His generosity and honesty in giving
questions.explanation: Bali is remembered above all for his boundless generosity and unbroken honesty.

questions.q: Which three realms had King Bali conquered before Vamana came to him?
questions.explanation: Bali had conquered the three worlds through devotion and sacrifice.

questions.q: In which sacred text is the story of King Bali and Vamana most fully told?
questions.explanation: The Vamana-Bali episode is narrated most fully in the Bhagavata Purana.

questions.q: When Vamana grew vast, in how many steps did he cover all of creation?
questions.explanation: Vamana covered all the worlds in just two of his three promised steps.

questions.q: What clan or lineage of Asuras did King Bali belong to?
questions.explanation: Bali was a Daitya king, descended from the line of Diti and Hiranyakashipu.

questions.q: Whose throne in heaven did King Bali seize after conquering the three worlds?
questions.explanation: Bali displaced Indra and took dominion over Svarga, the heaven of the gods.

questions.q: Which guru of the Asuras warned King Bali that the dwarf seeking land was Vishnu in disguise?
questions.explanation: Shukracharya, preceptor of the Asuras, warned Bali not to grant the request.

questions.q: Where did Vamana place his second step when measuring out the three paces?
questions.explanation: With one step Vamana spanned the earth and with the second the sky and heavens.

questions.q: Having covered all creation in two steps, where did Vamana place his third step?
questions.explanation: With nowhere left to step, Bali offered his own head for Vamana's third pace.

questions.q: What grand ritual was King Bali performing when Vamana arrived to ask for land?
questions.explanation: Bali was conducting a great sacrificial yajna when the dwarf came begging.

questions.q: When Bali poured the ritual water to seal his gift, how did Shukracharya try to stop it?
options: By entering the spout to block it
questions.explanation: Shukracharya shrank and entered the vessel's spout to block the water; Vamana cleared it with a blade of grass, which cost the guru one eye.

questions.q: Why did Vishnu act to humble King Bali despite Bali's righteousness?
options: To restore Indra and the gods to heaven
questions.explanation: The gods, led by Indra, appealed to Vishnu to recover the heavens Bali had won.

questions.q: According to tradition, who pleaded with Vamana on Bali's behalf after he was bound?
questions.explanation: Prahlada and others interceded, and Vishnu honored Bali for keeping his word.

questions.q: What boon did Vishnu grant King Bali as a reward for his unbroken truthfulness?
options: A place in Vaikuntha at once
Lordship of Patala and Vishnu as his gatekeeper
questions.explanation: Vishnu gave Bali lordship of Patala and promised to guard his door himself.

questions.q: The harvest festival celebrated as the annual return of King Bali to his people is best known by which name?
questions.explanation: In Kerala the festival of Onam commemorates the yearly return of King Bali to visit his subjects.

questions.q: Vamana is counted as which descent among Vishnu's traditional ten avatars (Dashavatara)?
questions.explanation: Vamana, who humbled Bali, is the fifth of Vishnu's ten avatars.

questions.q: What did King Bali do when he realized the dwarf was Vishnu himself coming to take all he had?
options: He kept his promise and offered all gladly
questions.explanation: Bali honored his word without hesitation, offering everything including himself.

questions.q: In Bali's lineage, Prahlada was the son of which demon king slain by Narasimha?
questions.explanation: Prahlada, Bali's grandfather, was the son of Hiranyakashipu, whom Narasimha slew.

questions.q: By what patronymic, derived from his father Virochana, is King Bali sometimes known?
questions.explanation: As the son of Virochana, Bali is sometimes called Vairochani.

questions.q: Many traditions name King Bali as one of the Chiranjivis. What does that term mean?
questions.explanation: Bali is counted among the Chiranjivis, beings granted exceptionally long or undying life.

questions.q: Some Puranic accounts foretell that King Bali will eventually hold which exalted office?
options: A future Indra in a coming age
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that Bali will become Indra, king of the gods, in a future cosmic age.

questions.q: When Vamana set his expanding foot upon the heavens, whose water is said to have washed it, giving rise to a sacred river?
questions.explanation: In Puranic lore Brahma washed Vamana's foot, and that water became the heavenly Ganga.

questions.q: The episode in which Bali surrenders the worlds is often called by which name in the Puranas?
questions.explanation: The story is known as the Vamana avatara, Vishnu's dwarf incarnation before Bali.

questions.q: Who was the father of King Bali in the Daitya lineage?
questions.explanation: Bali was the son of Virochana, himself the son of Prahlada.

questions.q: In the great enumeration of avatars, the Vamana descent that humbled Bali bridges which two ages of Vishnu's forms?
options: The sea avatars and the sky avatars
The animal avatars and the fully human avatars
The warrior and the sage avatars
questions.explanation: Vamana, the dwarf who became a man, marks Vishnu's passage from animal forms to human avatars.
